intro: Cloudinary enters at $0/month against Amazon S3 at $5.87 per month. Both tools position themselves for image/video heavy applications, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Amazon S3 — Object storage service with 11 nines durability, S3 Select, versioning, and lifecycle management. Cloudinary — Media management platform with image/video upload, on-the-fly transformation, CDN delivery, and AI tagging. Amazon S3 carries medium lock-in (S3 API became de facto standard; data egress costly) while Cloudinary is high (Transforms and URLs tightly coupled). Amazon S3 rates 4/5 on transparency versus 3/5 for Cloudinary. Both tools list image/video heavy applications as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including image/video heavy applications. The honest trade-off: Amazon S3 is not built for small projects, and Cloudinary is not the right call for small projects, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: Amazon S3 is for image/video heavy applications; Cloudinary overlaps on the same job; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amazon S3 if image/video heavy applications and the $5.87 per month entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Cloudinary if image/video heavy applications and the $0/month entry price fits your budget.

faq.q: Is Amazon S3 cheaper than Cloudinary?
faq.a: Based on the listed entry prices, no: Amazon S3 starts at $5.87 per month while Cloudinary starts at $0/month. At low volume the gap may be small, but it widens fast at scale. Always price against your own usage curve, not the headline tier.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amazon S3 to Cloudinary?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Amazon S3 is moderate effort at medium lock-in (S3 API became de facto standard; data egress costly), and onboarding to Cloudinary commits you to high lock-in (Transforms and URLs tightly coupled). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Amazon S3 or Cloudinary?
faq.a: Both Amazon S3 and Cloudinary score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis alone. The real differentiator usually lands in SDK fit for your language, error handling, and docs depth. A half-day integration spike tells you more than the score.

faq.q: Is Cloudinary a good alternative to Amazon S3?
faq.a: Yes, Cloudinary targets the same job Amazon S3 does — image/video heavy applications — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (subscription from $0/month vs subscription from $5.87 per month) and lock-in (high lock-in (Transforms and URLs tightly coupled) vs medium lock-in (S3 API became de facto standard; data egress costly)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Cloudinary is explicitly not for small projects.
